data.title: Rivian Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Analyst Sees Scaling Momentum, Strong Liquidity and Confirms $22 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst George Gianarikas of Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on Rivian Automotive, retaining the price target of $22.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

George Gianarikas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Rivian’s scaling strategy, product strength, and funding visibility. He views the company’s decision to expand initial capacity at the Georgia plant to 300,000 mid-sized vehicles annually as a clear signal that Rivian is preparing for meaningful volume, cost efficiencies, and long-term growth, supported by a restructured DOE loan and an unchanged $22 price target.

He also highlights Rivian’s sizable $14 billion liquidity position, reinforced by expected contributions from partners like Volkswagen and Uber, as a key buffer on the path to profitability. Combined with strong reception for the R1 and the disruptive potential of the R2 in a U.S. market lacking a clear non-Tesla EV leader, he believes Rivian is well placed to emerge as the leading mass-market challenger as EV adoption and consumer interest improve.

In another report released today, Benchmark Co. also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $25.00 price target.

RIVN’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $12.990 to $16.400, which is a 26.25% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
